items.title: Critical vulnerability in Amasty extensions leading to Remote Code Execution
items.summary: A critical vulnerability affecting 25 Amasty extensions was disclosed, which could lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, which can lead to remote code execution via a crafted cookie. CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe Commerce versions 2.4.9-beta1 and earlier are affected by an Incorrect Authorization vulnerability that could result in a security feature bypass and unauthorized write access without user interaction.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce. Specific details regarding the vulnerabilities resolved are available in the full security bulletin.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered the PolyShell vulnerability, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, potentially leading to remote code execution and account takeover. Mass exploitation attempts have been observed.

items.title: Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update addressing multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, which could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Adobe recommends applying patches immediately.
